title: Ensure That Cloud Audit Logging Is Configured Properly

input_row.title: Ensure That Cloud Audit Logging Is Configured Properly
input_row.description: It is recommended that Cloud Audit Logging is configured to track all admin activities and read, write access to user data.
input_row.rationale: Cloud Audit Logging maintains two audit logs for each project, folder, and organization: Admin Activity and Data Access. 1. Admin Activity logs contain log entries for API calls or other administrative actions that modify the configuration or metadata of resources. Admin Activity audit logs are enabled for all services and cannot be configured. 2. Data Access audit logs record API calls that create, modify, or read user- provided data. These are disabled by default and should be enabled. There are three kinds of Data Access audit log information: o Admin read: Records operations that read metadata or configuration information. Admin Activity audit logs record writes of metadata and configuration information that cannot be disabled. o Data read: Records operations that read user-provided data. o Data write: Records operations that write user-provided data. It is recommended to have an effective default audit config configured in such a way that: 1. logtype is set to DATA_READ (to log user activity tracking) and DATA_WRITES (to log changes/tampering to user data). 2. audit config is enabled for all the services supported by the Data Access audit logs feature. 3. Logs should be captured for all users, i.e., there are no exempted users in any of the audit config sections. This will ensure overriding the audit config will not contradict the requirement.
input_row.audit: From Google Cloud Console 1. Go to Audit Logs by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/iam-admin/audit. 2. Ensure that Admin Read, Data Write, and Data Read are enabled for all Google Cloud services and that no exemptions are allowed. From Google Cloud CLI 1. List the Identity and Access Management (IAM) policies for the project, folder, or organization: gcloud organizations get-iam-policy ORGANIZATION_ID gcloud resource-manager folders get-iam-policy FOLDER_ID gcloud projects get-iam-policy PROJECT_ID 2. Policy should have a default auditConfigs section which has the logtype set to DATA_WRITES and DATA_READ for all services. Note that projects inherit settings from folders, which in turn inherit settings from the organization. When called, projects get-iam-policy, the result shows only the policies set in the project, not the policies inherited from the parent folder or organization. Nevertheless, if the parent folder has Cloud Audit Logging enabled, the project does as well. Sample output for default audit configs may look like this: auditConfigs: - auditLogConfigs: - logType: ADMIN_READ - logType: DATA_WRITE - logType: DATA_READ service: allServices 3. Any of the auditConfigs sections should not have parameter "exemptedMembers:" set, which will ensure that Logging is enabled for all users and no user is exempted.
input_row.remediation: From Google Cloud Console 1. Go to Audit Logs by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/iam-admin/audit. 2. Follow the steps at https://cloud.google.com/logging/docs/audit/configure-data- access to enable audit logs for all Google Cloud services. Ensure that no exemptions are allowed. From Google Cloud CLI 1. To read the project's IAM policy and store it in a file run a command: gcloud projects get-iam-policy PROJECT_ID > /tmp/project_policy.yaml Alternatively, the policy can be set at the organization or folder level. If setting the policy at the organization level, it is not necessary to also set it for each folder or project. gcloud organizations get-iam-policy ORGANIZATION_ID > /tmp/org_policy.yaml gcloud resource-manager folders get-iam-policy FOLDER_ID > /tmp/folder_policy.yaml 2. Edit policy in /tmp/policy.yaml, adding or changing only the audit logs configuration to: Note: Admin Activity Logs are enabled by default, and cannot be disabled. So they are not listed in these configuration changes. auditConfigs: - auditLogConfigs: - logType: DATA_WRITE - logType: DATA_READ service: allServices Note: exemptedMembers: is not set as audit logging should be enabled for all the users 3. To write new IAM policy run command: gcloud organizations set-iam-policy ORGANIZATION_ID /tmp/org_policy.yaml gcloud resource-manager folders set-iam-policy FOLDER_ID /tmp/folder_policy.yaml gcloud projects set-iam-policy PROJECT_ID /tmp/project_policy.yaml If the preceding command reports a conflict with another change, then repeat these steps, starting with the first step.
input_row.impact: There is no charge for Admin Activity audit logs. Enabling the Data Access audit logs might result in your project being charged for the additional logs usage.
input_row.default_value: Admin Activity logs are always enabled. They cannot be disabled. Data Access audit logs are disabled by default because they can be quite large.
input_row.additional_information: • Log type DATA_READ is equally important to that of DATA_WRITE to track detailed user activities. • BigQuery Data Access logs are handled differently from other data access logs. BigQuery logs are enabled by default and cannot be disabled. They do not count against logs allotment and cannot result in extra logs charges.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
The audit control can be completely verified through GCP APIs and can be utilized using Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) and GCP CLI commands. Since the audit process involves checking IAM policies that contain audit configurations, it is entirely feasible to automate.

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_logging_audit_logging_configuration_correctness

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
1. **GCP API calls**: Use `gcloud` commands to retrieve IAM policy configurations for specific projects, folders, or organizations.
   - Commands:
     - `gcloud projects get-iam-policy PROJECT_ID`
     - `gcloud resource-manager folders get-iam-policy FOLDER_ID`
     - `gcloud organizations get-iam-policy ORGANIZATION_ID`

2. **Configuration Validations**:
   - Check for the presence of an `auditConfigs` section in the IAM policy.
   - Verify that the `auditLogConfigs` contains both `logType: DATA_WRITE` and `logType: DATA_READ` for all Google Cloud services.
   - Ensure there are no `exemptedMembers` fields in any of the `auditConfigs` sections.

3. **Resources to enumerate**: Assess IAM policies across projects, folders, and organization levels.

4. **Pass/Fail Criteria**:
   - **Pass**: The IAM policy contains an `auditConfigs` section configured properly with both `DATA_WRITE` and `DATA_READ`, and no `exemptedMembers` sections.
   - **Fail**: If the `auditConfigs` section is absent, misconfigured (missing log types), or contains any `exemptedMembers`.

5. **Example**: The command `gcloud projects get-iam-policy PROJECT_ID` should return an output that includes:
   ```yaml
   auditConfigs:
   - auditLogConfigs:
     - logType: ADMIN_READ
     - logType: DATA_WRITE
     - logType: DATA_READ
     service: allServices
   ```
   Confirm that any configuration found adheres to the above standards for compliance verification.
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE

- **Manual vs Automated Classification:** The classification as "Automated" is correct. The control can be effectively audited using GCP APIs and Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) commands, which allow for programmatic access to IAM policies and audit configurations.
- **GCP APIs/Google Cloud SDK (gcloud)/CLI:** The use of `gcloud` commands to retrieve IAM policy configurations is appropriate and feasible for automation. The commands listed are valid for accessing IAM policies at the project, folder, and organization levels.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 8

- **Automation Details:** The automation details are mostly accurate. The `gcloud` commands are correctly specified for retrieving IAM policies. However, the assessment should ensure that the `auditConfigs` section is checked for all relevant services, not just `allServices`, as there might be specific services that require explicit configuration.
- **Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) Methods or CLI Commands:** The commands are valid, but the assessment should also consider using `gcloud logging sinks list` to verify that logs are being exported to a secure destination, which is a part of comprehensive audit logging.
- **Program Name:** The program name `gcp_logging_audit_logging_configuration_correctness` is descriptive but could be more concise. It should reflect the specific aspect of audit logging being checked, such as `gcp_logging_audit_config_verification`.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**

- **Missing Considerations:** The assessment should include checks for the presence of logging sinks to ensure logs are being exported to a secure and compliant destination, such as a Cloud Storage bucket, BigQuery dataset, or Pub/Sub topic.
- **Alternative GCP API Methods:** Consider using the `gcloud logging sinks` commands to verify that logs are being exported correctly.
- **Better Program Name Suggestion:** `gcp_logging_audit_config_verification` would be a more concise and clear name, focusing on the verification of audit configurations.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

- The assessment is well-structured and utilizes appropriate GCP tools and commands to automate the audit process. The technical details are mostly accurate, and the approach aligns with GCP security best practices.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:** Modify

- **Modify:** Include additional checks for logging sinks to ensure that logs are being exported to a secure destination. Update the program name to `gcp_logging_audit_config_verification` for clarity and conciseness.
- The current approach is solid, but these modifications will enhance the comprehensiveness and clarity of the audit process.

step3_final_decision.decision_text:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:**
Both AI opinions agree that the control can be effectively audited using GCP APIs and Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) commands. The audit involves checking IAM policies for audit configurations, which can be programmatically accessed and verified through the `gcloud` commands. This makes the process suitable for automation, as the necessary data is accessible via GCP APIs.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:** 
gcp_logging_audit_logging_configuration_verification

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- GCP Resource/Service: IAM Policies
- API/CLI Method: `gcloud projects get-iam-policy`, `gcloud resource-manager folders get-iam-policy`, `gcloud organizations get-iam-policy`
- Properties to Check: Presence of `auditConfigs` with `auditLogConfigs` containing `logType: DATA_WRITE` and `logType: DATA_READ`, and absence of `exemptedMembers`.
- Pass Criteria: The IAM policy contains an `auditConfigs` section configured with both `DATA_WRITE` and `DATA_READ`, and no `exemptedMembers`.
- Fail Criteria: Missing `auditConfigs` section, misconfigured log types, or presence of `exemptedMembers`.

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:**
There were no significant disagreements between the AI opinions; both supported automation. The second opinion suggested a more concise program name, which has been adopted.

**SOURCE:** CIS_Google_Cloud_Platform_Foundation_Benchmark_v4_0_0
